title: HuBMAP: Embedding Fresh Frozen OCT Samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e5v0s","text":"The purpose of this Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to outline procedures for the OCT embedding of HuBMAP fresh frozen specimens.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"b5ul2","text":"Managers and supervisors - are responsible for making sure that technicians are properly trained and equipment and facility are maintained in good working order.  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5b7th","text":"Laboratory personnel - are responsible for reading and understanding this SOP and related documents and to perform these tasks in accordance with the SOPs.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"qkue","text":"Ensure you have proper scalpel blades, forceps, and your personal preference of gauzes/wipes.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7j0b4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"es06o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e0gj5","text":"Embedding can be a messy process, to protect your clothes it is best to wear a lab coat or apron.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1ekqv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4gubt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e2q62","text":"Gloves are required when working with human specimens.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3tdpo","text":"Avoid direct skin contact with dry ice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"452sk","text":"Methylbutane should be collectedd as hazardous waste.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Collect tissue specimen from the designated common coordinate framework sites. (See case processing SOP diagrams) Appendix for Spleen SOP by Marda Jorgensen SOP Appendix for Spleen by Marda Jorgensen SOP Appendix for Thymus by Marda Jorgensen

Prepare a pre-labeled Cryomold Tissue Tek Cryomold (25mmx20mmx5mm) Contributed by users Catalog #25608-916 and fill it half way with OCT compound. Tissue-Tek® O.C.T. Compound, Sakura® Finetek VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #25608-930
Place the tissue into the OCT-containing Cryomold, maintaining tissue orientation.
Using forceps, push the tissue lightly to the bottom of the Cryomold to secure it.
Prepare dry ice/methylbutane slurry for freezing OCT blocks.
Fill the mold with OCT, covering tissue completely. Avoid bubbles.
Place 1-2 inches of dry ice pellets into the bolltom of an ice bucket or styrofoam box.
Add enough 2-methylbutane solution to cover the dry ice by roughly 5mm.
Place lid on freezing chamber and allow methylbutane to chill. Chamber is ready when fog dissipates and bottom of bucket becomes visible.

When the OCT Cryomold is thoroughly frozen and opaque, wrap the mold containing the tissue in a pre-labeled aluminum foil square and store at -80° C in a freezer rack box. Enter block location if Freezer Log for future retrieval.
Gently blot tissue piece on dry gauze to remove any excess moisture.

Freeze the tissue in the Cryomold by resting it on the surface of the methylbutane slurry. Use long handled forceps to transfer the mold onto the slurry. As the OCT inside the Cryomold begins to freeze, lightly push the tissue into the bottom of the mold one last time.
creator: Franchesca Farris
